title: Coespeletia timotensis
intro: Coespeletia timotensis, also known as the "Andean tea tree," is a shrub native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and improve overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Coespeletia timotensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments and promote health.
taxonomy: Coespeletia timotensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to Peru. The classification of Coespeletia timotensis is as follows: it belongs to the genus Coespeletia and the family Asteraceae. This species is considered to be a part of the Helianthodae clade, which includes daisy-like plants. Further classification details include its being a part of the subtribe Helianthinae.
